_meta.purpose: Master knowledge base for trap.lol. This file lives at status/logs/logs.json in the root of the trap.lol repository and is viewable at trap.lol/status/logs/. It is the single source of truth for any LLM, AI agent, or new context window that needs to understand this project — its history, architecture, philosophy, rules, aesthetic, goals, and the mind behind it. It is machine-readable AND human-readable. It must be read in full before making any decisions about this codebase.

_protocol.what_this_file_is: This is status/logs/logs.json in the trap.lol repository — the site's living intelligence record. It is not documentation. It is not a README. It is the site's memory — the full context a new LLM or context window needs to operate correctly without any input from the owner (Tristan Phillips / trap). It contains the site's identity, architecture, aesthetic rules, decision history, commit log, session record, and a set of binding laws for any agent working on this codebase. The human-readable viewer is at trap.lol/status/logs/.
_protocol.your_obligation_as_an_llm: If you are an LLM or AI agent reading this file: you are REQUIRED to update it as part of your session. This is not optional. This file becomes stale if it is not maintained. A stale file is worse than no file — it will mislead the next agent. Your obligation is: READ before you act, UPDATE before you commit.
when_to_update: After EVERY session where any of the following occurred: a file was changed, a commit was made, a decision was made, a rule was established or changed, a feature was added or removed, a philosophy was clarified.
Even if you only answered a question and made no code changes — if the question revealed something about trap's thinking or preferences, add it to the sessions array.
Do NOT update if the session was purely read-only with no new information revealed.

how_to_update_each_section._meta.last_updated: Set to today's date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Always.
how_to_update_each_section._meta.schema_version: Increment patch (1.0.X) for content updates. Increment minor (1.X.0) if you add a new top-level section. Increment major (X.0.0) only if the entire schema changes.
how_to_update_each_section.commits: Prepend a new entry to the front of the array for each commit made this session. Required fields: hash (7-char), date (YYYY-MM-DD), message (exact git commit message), scope (which parts of the site changed), impact (critical|high|medium|low), files (array of files changed), why (one sentence explaining the reason — not what, WHY).
how_to_update_each_section.sessions: Prepend a new entry to the front of the array. Required fields: date (YYYY-MM-DD), agent (model name + version), type (one of: pre_production_audit | feature_development | bug_fix | refactor | content | security | discussion | maintenance), summary (1-3 sentences describing what happened), decisions (array of key decisions made or things learned), rules_reinforced (array of rules that were confirmed or clarified this session).
how_to_update_each_section.evolution.current_state: Update this string if the site's overall state has meaningfully changed.
how_to_update_each_section.evolution.phases: Add a new phase entry if a clearly new chapter of development has begun. Don't add a phase for every session.
how_to_update_each_section.evolution.intended_future: Update if trap expressed new intentions or removed old ones.
how_to_update_each_section.pages: Add, update, or remove page entries when pages are created, modified, or deleted.
how_to_update_each_section.rules.absolute: Add new rules only if trap explicitly stated a hard rule. Do not infer rules — only record what was explicitly said or demonstrated through repeated preference.
how_to_update_each_section.rules.strong_preferences: Same as above but for preferences (not hard rules).
how_to_update_each_section.anti_patterns: Add to things_that_have_been_tried_and_rejected when something is removed or reversed. Add to common_mistakes_to_avoid when a class of error is identified.
how_to_update_each_section.language_fingerprint: Update recurring_words_and_phrases if new vocabulary is consistently used. Add to vocabulary_never_used if trap explicitly rejects a word. Update tone/style descriptions if they become more precise.

_protocol.staleness_detection: If _meta.last_updated is more than 7 days ago, flag this to the user before starting work. The file may be significantly out of date. Ask if they want to do a sync pass before proceeding.
validation_rules: commits[].impact must be one of: critical, high, medium, low
sessions[].type must be one of: pre_production_audit, feature_development, bug_fix, refactor, content, security, discussion, maintenance
All dates must be YYYY-MM-DD format
commit hashes must be exactly 7 characters
Do NOT remove or rewrite existing entries — only prepend new ones or update specific named fields
Do NOT change the schema structure — add fields only, never remove
_protocol.for_claude_code_specifically: When working in Claude Code (CLI): at session start, read status/logs/logs.json (this file, at the root of the trap.lol repo). At session end, before committing, update commits[] and sessions[] in status/logs/logs.json. The commit entry for the logs update itself should list files: ['status/logs/logs.json'] with scope 'logs' and impact 'low'. Do not create a separate commit just for the logs file — include it in the most relevant commit for the session, or as the last commit if the session had multiple commits.

identity.author: trap
identity.what_it_is: A personal sovereign infrastructure dashboard. A command centre. A gallery. A playground. A signal hub. It is not a portfolio. It is not a blog. It is an operating environment — a single point of truth for everything trap owns, runs, thinks, and creates.
what_it_is_not: Not built for other people's aesthetics
Not React, Vue, Angular, or any framework

philosophy.core_belief: Sovereignty. Everything runs on trap's own infrastructure, under trap's own domain, under trap's own rules. No third-party platforms, no vendor lock-in, no compromise on control.
philosophy.design_philosophy: Zero build steps. Zero frameworks. Zero server-side processing. Pure vanilla HTML, CSS, JS. Flat JSON as the database. The site must be deployable by dropping files on any static host. Complexity lives in the data layer and the prose, not in the toolchain.
philosophy.aesthetic_philosophy: Dune × Arcane. Desert-night chaos. The palette is deep violet, gold, magenta, teal — never pastel, never corporate, never default. Everything has a glow. Everything has a grain. The UI feels like a terminal from a science fiction universe that takes itself seriously.
philosophy.operational_philosophy: Assume Breach. Stay Sovereign. Everything is monitored. Everything is logged. Shortcuts are typed, not clicked. The site rewards power users.
philosophy.content_philosophy: Content is data. Nothing is hardcoded into HTML. Every string, every link, every card is driven by JSON. If you want to add a service, you edit a JSON file. The HTML is purely structural scaffolding.
philosophy.llm_philosophy: LLMs are tools, not replacements. The playground exists because AI augments trap's thinking — it doesn't replace it. The system prompt is terse, direct, no disclaimers. The models are sovereign (self-hosted routing via nano-gpt, never OpenAI direct when possible).
philosophy.evolution_philosophy: This site evolves. Features are added when they serve a real need, removed when they don't. The guides section has been commented out repeatedly — it may return. The gacha pull mechanic was added not because galleries needed it but because the experience should be unexpected. Never be boring. Never be predictable. But never add noise for its own sake.

stack.html: Vanilla HTML5. Semantic structure. No templating.
stack.css: Vanilla CSS. BEM naming. CSS custom properties for theming. No Tailwind, no SASS, no PostCSS.
stack.js: Vanilla ES modules. No bundler. No transpiler. Import paths are relative. Runs directly in the browser.
stack.data: Flat JSON files in glass/data/. No database. No API. No server.
stack.cdn: Self-hosted at cdn.trap.lol. Lucide icons, marked.js, DOMPurify. Never load from npm CDNs in production.
stack.hosting: GitHub Pages. Zero config. Push to main = deployed.

boot_sequence.description: The app boots in a strict sequence. Breaking the sequence breaks the app.
steps: 1. Fetch glass/data/config.json (BLOCKING — contains CDN shard URLs needed for step 2)
2. Parallel: fetch all data JSONs + inject lucide shard script tag into head
3. Render each section by calling render*() functions which write innerHTML into pre-existing container divs
4. lucide.createIcons() called ONCE after ALL HTML injection is complete
boot_sequence.critical_rule: lucide.createIcons() must only be called AFTER all HTML is injected. Calling it mid-render leaves unprocessed <i data-lucide> tags. Exception: clipboard icon swap and pull zone tier icon — these use lucide.createIcons({ nodes: [...] }) scoped to specific elements.

data_layer.config.json: Site metadata (title, domain, tagline, eyebrow, author, year), CDN shard URLs (lucide, marked, dompurify), LLM config (api_base, api_token, system_prompt), referral codes, social links.
data_layer.hosting.json: Sovereign Nodes section. Self-hosted services. Each entry has: name, hosting (the software), shortcut (2-char unique), icon (lucide name), url, status (online|offline), optional source (git repo URL).
data_layer.bots.json: Drone Fleet section. Telegram bots. Each entry has: name, shortcut, icon, chat_url (nullable), repo_url, status, description.
data_layer.tools.json: Ordnance Depot section. CLI tools/payloads. Each entry has: name, shortcut, icon, description, target (OS), repo_url, install_cmd (the thing the shortcut copies).
data_layer.extlinks.json: Signal Mesh section. External links grouped by category. Each category has: domain, category, visual_class (neon-*), manifest of links with: name, shortcut, url, trust_level (Vetted|High), info.
data_layer.guides.json: Sacred Codex (currently commented out). Array of guide entries: title, description, icon, file (markdown filename), read_time.
data_layer.llm.json: LLM routing table. Keys are model IDs. Each model has: label, provider, pay_per_token (bool). Also contains default_routing.
data_layer.apps.json: App Uplinks section. Each app has: name, slug, path, icon, description, tags[], keyed (bool — requires API key), status (live|planned).
data_layer.art/glass/data/detention.json: ECHOES gallery data. Collections of artwork artifacts. Each artifact has: compositeId (collectionId::artifactId), src.small (thumbnail URL path), src.original (full URL path), uploadDate, tags[], type, metadata (medium, description, verse, mood, chaos). baseUrl in meta is prepended to all src paths.
data_layer.status/logs/logs.json: THIS FILE. The site intelligence record. Updated every session by the working LLM. See _protocol for update rules.

shortcut_system.description: The globalRouter (Map) is populated during render. Every item with a shortcut field registers a 2-char keystroke. On desktop (non-touch), typing 2 chars triggers the action.
rules: Shortcuts must be globally unique across ALL JSON files
Buffer auto-resets after 2s idle or on match

module_structure.glass/script/main.js: Entry point. Calls loadCoreData() then renderUI(). DOMContentLoaded guard.
module_structure.glass/script/modules/core.js: fetchJSON(), loadScript(), esc(), renderError(), loadCoreData() — fetches config.json then all data JSONs in parallel, loads shards. Exports globalRouter, IS_TOUCH, all data variables.
module_structure.glass/script/modules/ui.js: All render functions: renderMeta, renderHeader, renderFooter, renderHosting, renderTools, renderBots, renderSignalMesh, renderCodex (guarded — guides section commented out), renderApps, renderUplinkLaunchpad, renderSocialCluster. All event handlers: initClipboard, initBotTooltips, initScrollTop, initExtTooltips, initTerminal, initFlipCards. updateCarouselArrows.
module_structure.glass/script/modules/llm.js: LLM playground orchestrator. Wires up all UI refs, manages state (history, turnCount, tokenCount), handles form submit, agent roster async loading.
module_structure.glass/script/modules/llm-chat.js: Message rendering, markdown sanitization (DOMPurify + marked), streaming response handler, clipboard copy for code blocks.
module_structure.glass/script/modules/llm-sessions.js: Session save/load/export (JSON + MD), bulk delete, import.
module_structure.glass/script/modules/llm-settings.js: Model selector, system prompt override, temperature, context window, streaming toggle, enter-to-send toggle.
module_structure.glass/script/modules/tunnel-light.js: The animated tunnel light effect on the home page header.
module_structure.glass/data/llm-agents/: Per-agent JSON files. index.json lists filenames. Each agent file has spec + data (name, tags, system_prompt, temperature, model override).
module_structure.status/logs/index.html: The intelligence terminal viewer. Reads logs.json and renders it. Contains a chat widget that accepts a nano-gpt API key (localStorage) and lets you query the knowledge base in natural language.
module_structure.status/logs/logs.json: The knowledge base itself. See _protocol for schema and update obligations.

/.title: trap.lol // uplink
/.purpose: The hub. Everything starts here. Renders all sections from JSON data.

/art/.title: trap.lol // echoes
/art/.purpose: ECHOES gallery. Gacha pull mechanic reveals artwork in rarity tiers. Lightbox with full zoom, pan, rotate, flip. Filter by medium (all / sketch / digital).
/art/.script: art/glass/script/app.js (type=module, self-contained — does NOT use glass/script/)
/art/.pity_system: After 4 consecutive sub-CIPHER pulls: force min CIPHER. After 7 consecutive sub-SPICE pulls: force min SPICE. Never telegraphed to user.

/playground/.title: trap.lol // neural uplink
/playground/.purpose: LLM playground. Multi-model chat interface. Session management, agent personas, system prompt override, temperature control, streaming.
/playground/.auth: API key stored in localStorage. Key never sent anywhere except the API endpoint.
/playground/.models: Defined in glass/data/llm.json routing_table. Includes DeepSeek, Claude, GPT, Llama, Qwen, Gemini, Mistral, GLM.
/playground/.agents: JSON files in glass/data/llm-agents/. Loaded async. Default agent = default.json.

/playground/characters/.title: The Underdark // Reality Matrix
/playground/characters/.purpose: Advanced roleplay interface. Cinematic multi-bot layout. Character creation, lorebooks, scenario editor. The 'Underdark' is the deepest, most immersive LLM experience on the site.

/radar/.title: RADAR // ANOMALY
/radar/.purpose: Torrent scanner and TorBox debrid cache interface. Search for torrents, check TorBox cache, queue downloads. Requires TorBox API key.
/radar/.api: TorBox API. Key stored in localStorage.

/status/.title: trap.lol // status
/status/.purpose: Uplink Sentinel. Live infrastructure monitor. Probes sovereign nodes and app uplinks every 60 seconds. Shows response time, sparkline history, online/offline badge. Drone fleet is static (Telegram bots — not HTTP-probeable). Favicon and tab title update to reflect overall system state.
/status/.probe_method: nocors fetch for external nodes (opaque response = up), same-origin fetch for apps.

/status/logs/.title: trap.lol // logs
/status/logs/.purpose: Site intelligence terminal. Reads logs.json and renders the complete evolution history of the site — commits, sessions, design decisions, architectural rules, philosophy, page manifest, and a briefing for any new LLM or context window. Includes a chat widget (nano-gpt API key required) to query the knowledge base in natural language. The definitive 'what is this, how does it work, and where is it going' document.
/status/logs/.note: The chat widget stores the API key in localStorage under 'logs_ng_key'. Messages are sent to the nano-gpt /v1/chat/completions endpoint with the full logs.json injected as system context. Client-side only — no server, no persistence.

/guide/.title: trap.lol // docs
/guide/.purpose: Markdown reader. Reads ?md= param, fetches from /glass/markdown/<filename>, renders via marked.js + DOMPurify sanitization. Shares glass/style/style.css.
/guide/.note: The Sacred Codex section on the home page (guides.json + carousel) is currently commented out in index.html but all backend logic (renderCodex, guides.json) is maintained. It will return.

palette.bg_base: #05030A / #06040A — near-black with a deep violet undertone. Never pure black.
palette.accent_primary: #BE29EC — electric violet/magenta. The dominant glow colour.
palette.accent_warm: #F4A830 — spice gold. Used for hover states, warm accents, the 'reach' colour.
palette.accent_rust: #C4562A — deep rust/orange. Used sparingly for depth.
palette.accent_cyan: #2dd4a0 — teal. Used in TRACE tier and cyan neon category.
palette.text_muted: #6A5C7A — muted violet-grey. Secondary text, labels, hints.
palette.text_warm: #C4A862 — warm tan. Used in verse, quotes, warm labels.

typography.mono: ui-monospace, Cascadia Code, Source Code Pro, Menlo, Consolas, monospace — used for ALL UI chrome: eyebrows, labels, badges, stats, code, shortcut keys, technical strings.
typography.sans: system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, Segoe UI, sans-serif — used for body text, descriptions.
typography.serif: Georgia, Times New Roman, serif — used for verse, italic pull quotes, poetic UI copy.
typography.rule: If it's UI chrome, it's mono. If it's prose, it's sans. If it's a verse or CTA italic, it's serif.

effects.glass: backdrop-filter: blur(12-24px) + rgba background + 1px solid border at low opacity. Every card, every panel.
effects.glow: box-shadow with 0 0 Xpx rgba(accent, 0.Y). Glows are never harsh. They bloom softly.
effects.grain: SVG feTurbulence filter as a fixed body::before overlay at 3% opacity. Subtle film grain across the entire page.
effects.scanlines: repeating-linear-gradient on body::after. 4px pattern at 2.2% opacity.
effects.sweep: Horizontal 1px gradient line that slides across card tops on hover. Left to right, warm accent colour.
effects.accent_bar: 2px left border on cards/sections with a vertical gradient from accent-warm to accent to transparent. Appears on hover or always on sections.
effects.tunnel_light: Animated perspective-corridor effect on the home page header. Only initialised on non-touch devices.
effects.cursor_orb: Custom cursor orb (ECHOES page). Fine pointer only. 13px circle, lerp-tracked at 0.13 speed. Expands to 24px ring on hover, 18px filled on press.
effects.particles: 52-particle canvas system (ECHOES page). Slow upward drift, palette colours, fade in/out lifecycle. Paused when tab hidden. Disabled on reduced-motion.

spacing.system: CSS custom properties: --space-xs through --space-xl. Consistent rhythm.

design_system.naming_convention: Strict BEM. Block__element--modifier. No exceptions in new CSS. No utility classes. No inline styles except JS-driven transforms and dynamic values.
animation_principles: All animations respect prefers-reduced-motion — kill or minimise when set
Enter animations: translateY(7-20px) to 0, opacity 0 to 1, 0.4-0.5s ease-out
Hover transitions: 0.18s ease-out for fast snappy feedback
State transitions: 0.3-0.5s cubic-bezier(0.25, 0, 0, 1) for smooth state changes
No spinning loaders — use opacity pulse or scale pulse instead

absolute: NEVER use innerHTML with unsanitized external data. Always use esc() for JSON data going into innerHTML. Use DOMPurify.sanitize() for markdown content.
NEVER call lucide.createIcons() mid-render. Only call it after ALL HTML injection is complete. Exception: scoped { nodes: [...] } calls for dynamic icon swaps.
NEVER use a framework. No React, Vue, Svelte, Angular, Lit, Solid, or anything that requires a build step.
NEVER use a bundler. No webpack, Vite, Rollup, Parcel, esbuild in the render pipeline.
NEVER load from npm CDNs (unpkg, jsDelivr, cdnjs) in production. All shards load from cdn.trap.lol first, fall back to local /shards/ for dev, then public CDN as last resort.
NEVER add comments explaining WHAT the code does. Only comment WHY if non-obvious. No multi-line docblocks. One short line max.
NEVER create documentation .md files unless explicitly requested.
NEVER add error handling for scenarios that cannot happen. Trust the data layer.
NEVER add features beyond what was asked. No scope creep. No 'while I'm here' additions.
NEVER commit sensitive files (.env, API keys, credentials).
NEVER break the BEM naming convention in CSS.
NEVER use pure black (#000) or pure white (#fff) for backgrounds/text — always the slightly-off palette values.
Shortcut keys must be exactly 2 chars, globally unique across all JSON files.
status values in hosting.json and bots.json must be 'online' or 'offline' only.
trust_level in extlinks.json must be 'Vetted' or 'High' only.
visual_class in extlinks.json categories must be one of the five defined neon-* classes.
ALWAYS update status/logs/logs.json at the end of every session where changes were made. This is non-negotiable. The file is the site's memory.
strong_preferences: Prefer editing existing files over creating new ones.
Prefer CSS custom properties over hardcoded values.

Prefer document.createElement + direct property setting over innerHTML for dynamic interactive elements (cards, tooltips). Use innerHTML template literals for static display-only content rendered once.
Prefer flat data structures. Avoid deeply nested JSON.
Prefer declarative render functions that are idempotent (can be re-called safely).
CSS should be mobile-first. Base styles are mobile. Desktop overrides go in min-width media queries.
All interactive elements must be keyboard accessible (tabindex, keydown Enter/Space handlers, aria-label).
Response times to external fetches should always have an AbortController with an 8s timeout.
code_style: No trailing comments on code lines.
Short functions. If a function is doing two things, split it.
Section separators use the style: /* ══ SECTION NAME ══ */ for major blocks.
Variable names are camelCase. Constants are UPPER_SNAKE for true constants, camelCase for module-level config.
Arrow functions for callbacks. function declarations for named top-level functions.
Prefer const, then let. Never var.

things_that_have_been_tried_and_rejected: Frameworks — rejected for the 'zero build step' principle
Service Workers — actively unregistered on every page load (core.js line 5-8). They caused stale cache issues during active development.
Uplink section on home page — removed because it duplicated the App Uplinks section
Guides carousel on home page — commented out, not deleted. Kept because it will return when there's enough guide content.
DOMPurify on the main dashboard — not needed there because all content is owner-controlled JSON, not user input. Only needed where markdown from files is rendered (guide page, playground).
common_mistakes_to_avoid: Adding esc() to values you're setting via textContent — esc() is only needed for innerHTML injection
Calling lucide.createIcons() without { nodes: [...] } scope after DOM is already fully rendered — this rescans everything unnecessarily
Setting innerHTML on elements that are never re-rendered with static text — use textContent instead
Adding loading spinners — use the existing CSS animation patterns (opacity pulse, dot-seek)
Re-observing already-visible cards in IntersectionObserver — use :not(.visible) selector
Exposing .backup, .bk, .env files in the public directory — gitignored but double-check
Adding path traversal vectors in URL param-driven fetch calls — always validate params with a strict regex whitelist
Letting logs.json go stale — it must be updated every session or the next agent starts blind

language_fingerprint.description: How trap writes, communicates, and thinks. Understanding this lets an LLM match the voice and intent.
recurring_words_and_phrases: uplink (means connection/access to LLM or service)
detention (the art gallery data file — not a prison, a holding space)
feel everything. regret nothing. (the echoes mantra)
language_fingerprint.tone: Terse. Precise. No preamble. No disclaimers. Commands, not suggestions. The UI copy reads like a military terminal crossed with a drug dealer's poetry — functional, cryptic, beautiful.
language_fingerprint.ui_copy_style: Short fragments. Mono font. ALL CAPS for labels and statuses. Lowercase italic for whispers and subtext. Never full sentences in UI chrome.
language_fingerprint.dune_references: Spice = rare/valuable things. Abyss = total immersion. The vocabulary is deliberately Dune-adjacent without being cosplay.
language_fingerprint.how_trap_thinks: Trap iterates fast. Changes commit messages are often one word ('changes', 'fixes', 'uiux'). But the code itself is highly intentional. The aesthetic is always consistent even when the feature is rough. Trap cares deeply about the experience but moves at speed. Don't over-explain. Don't slow down. Ship and refine.

evolution.description: How the site has changed over time, what drove those changes, and the direction of travel.

origin.description: Initial commit. The site was born as a personal infrastructure dashboard. The core idea — sovereign nodes, JSON-driven, no frameworks — was present from day one.

phases.description: Core dashboard established. Hosting nodes, signal mesh, tools, extlinks. Radar torrent scanner added. ECHOES art page created with initial gallery data. LLM playground first implementation.
Mobile UI improvements. Tunnel light effect added. Tooltip system. Social cluster added. Guide section hidden. Name wrapping fixes. Epilepsy-sensitive animation gating added.
LLM playground massively expanded. Character system added (The Underdark). Session management. Roleplay interface. Agent personas. Mobile optimisation of playground.
The Underdark (characters/roleplay) further developed. Char editor, image generator integration, scenario updates, persona system refined. ECHOES data refreshed with new collection.
key_commits: Char editor changes and image generator changes

phases.description: Pre-production security and quality audit. XSS fixes in guide page (DOMPurify + path validation). Art gallery performance fix (IntersectionObserver scoping). Backup file removal. Logs system created — the site's persistent memory. Protocol enforcement added so any future LLM agent knows how and when to update the knowledge base.
key_commits: Logs protocol, chat widget, and CLAUDE.md enforcement

evolution.current_state: Production-ready. All core features live. The logs system is now the site's persistent memory — a knowledge base that any LLM agent reads at session start and updates at session end. The main areas of active development are The Underdark (LLM roleplay) and ECHOES (art gallery).
intended_future: Sacred Codex (guides section) reactivation when enough .md content exists
More ECHOES collections as artwork is created/curated
ECHOES metadata enrichment (descriptions, verses, mood/chaos values populated)
Possible: RSS/activity feed section on home page
Possible: More sovereign nodes as self-hosting expands
Possible: Status page historical uptime tracking (persistent data)
Never: A backend. Never: A framework. Never: A CMS.

commits.message: Underdark overhaul: Scene Codex, video generation, expanded model list
commits.why: User requested a comprehensive immersion overhaul of The Underdark roleplay playground — full god-view panel, video generation, all subscription models accessible from the model picker, and deep RP text layer styling.

commits.message: Ground logs briefing with explicit file paths
commits.why: The briefing text and all protocol fields never named the file's path. A new LLM pasting the briefing had no way to know where to find or update the file without prior context.

commits.message: Replace shard-based markdown with inline renderer for chat
commits.why: CDN shard globals are not reliably accessible inside type=module scripts. Inline renderer has no dependencies and no timing risk.

commits.message: Render chat responses as markdown in logs oracle
commits.why: Chat responses were rendering raw markdown (asterisks, backticks) as plain text. Loaded marked + DOMPurify shards in parallel with boot fetch and added full markdown CSS for assistant bubbles.

commits.message: Fix logs page: hidden CSS, chat key state, Protocol tab
commits.why: .hidden had no CSS rule so nothing was hidden; chat widget had no key-absent state so it appeared broken; model string was wrong; Protocol tab was missing from UI.

commits.message: Logs protocol, chat widget, and session enforcement
commits.why: Add _protocol to logs.json (machine-readable update contract), chat widget to index.html (nano-gpt query interface), and session-start/end protocol to CLAUDE.md.
Create the site's persistent memory system — a JSON knowledge base and interactive viewer so any new LLM or context window can fully understand the site without manual briefing.

commits.message: Add CHANGELOG.md to track pre-production fixes
commits.why: Formalise the change record for future context.

Pre-production audit found XSS vector in guide page (marked.parse without DOMPurify), path traversal in ?md= param, redundant IntersectionObserver re-observation in art gallery, publicly served backup file. All fixed.
Status page (Uplink Sentinel) implemented. LLM playground updates.
Iterative development cycle.

commits.message: Char editor changes and image generator changes and handling
commits.why: The Underdark character editor and image generation integration.
Ongoing LLM and character system refinement.
Roleplay interface implementation — core of The Underdark.

commits.message: Art page thumbnail single column update
commits.why: Portrait images get 3/4 aspect ratio in single-column mobile layout.
Tunnel light visual effect on header. Bot tooltip system.
prefers-reduced-motion gating on all animations. Accessibility requirement.
Mobile layout pass — responsive improvements across home page.
Social cluster component added. Radial layout, nucleus button, 5 platform nodes.
Guides/Sacred Codex section commented out in index.html. Not enough content yet. Logic preserved.
LLM playground major iteration.
Router bug fix — shortcut key handling correction.

commits.message: radar changed, vps more universally dumb
commits.why: RADAR interface changes, VPS integration simplified.
The beginning.

sessions.summary: Comprehensive overhaul of playground/characters (The Underdark). Added: Scene Codex god-view panel (floating overlay with narrative meters, 6 god-control directives, quick image/video gen, scene digest), video generation feature (nano-gpt video API with polling, img2vid support, video messages in thread), expanded model list in llm.json to 40+ models (all nano-gpt subscription models + key paid-tier models), 3 new reinject directives (slow/climax/afterglow), immersive focus mode CSS polish, richer RP text layer styling (speech/thought/action visual layers), streaming cursor animation.
decisions: Scene Codex uses CSS transform animation (not display:none toggle) so the slide-down feels physical and grounded.
Narrative meters (tension/intimacy/danger) are heuristic-based — keyword scanning of last 10 messages. No LLM call needed, zero latency.
Video generation polls the task endpoint every 4s. Progress is estimated from elapsed time rather than true server progress (API does not return percentage).
img2vid: resolves last image message from state.history as the start frame. Gracefully falls back to text-to-video if no image found.
All new god-control directives (slow, climax, afterglow) added to REINJECT_LABELS — same system as existing reinject buttons, no new infrastructure.
Model list expansion uses the _index grouped display already in the UI. All 40+ models auto-appear in model select once llm.json is updated.
Video messages stored in state.history with role: 'video'. Rendered via _injectVideoMessage, same pattern as _injectImageMessage.
rules_reinforced: scene-codex.css overrides [hidden] { display: none !important } for .scene-codex specifically so animation can run via class toggle. This is intentional — do not revert.
Video generation is async/polling — never block the UI. Always show progress bar and status text.
Function declarations inside initUI() are hoisted — safe to call before their literal position in the file.

sessions.summary: Fixed critical bugs in status/logs/index.html: .hidden class had no CSS rule (everything showed simultaneously), chat widget had no key-absent state (looked broken/endless), model string was wrong. Added Protocol tab. Hardened copy-briefing text to include sixth law and obligation text.
decisions: .hidden must be defined in each page's own <style> — it is not in the shared glass/style/style.css. Added .hidden { display: none !important } to logs page.
Chat no-key state: amber banner + disabled textarea + focus to key input. After saving key: focus shifts to textarea.
nano-gpt model ID for DeepSeek R1 is 'deepseek-r1' not 'deepseek/deepseek-r1'.
Protocol tab added so _protocol is browsable in the UI — not just machine-read. The obligation and field-update specs are now visible to human users too.
Copy-briefing text now includes the sixth law, your_obligation_as_an_llm, when_to_update, and staleness_detection — so the copied text is a complete LLM briefing.
rules_reinforced: .hidden is not a global utility class in this project — define it per page if needed
Always test the no-key state of any API-dependent widget before shipping

sessions.summary: Added _protocol section to logs.json establishing machine-readable update obligations for any LLM working on this codebase. Added chat widget to status/logs/index.html (nano-gpt API key, queries logs.json as context). Updated CLAUDE.md with mandatory session-start/end log obligations. Added the logs update rule to rules.absolute.
decisions: _protocol section is the enforcement mechanism — it tells any LLM what to update, when, how, and in what format. Placed first in reading_order so it is seen before any data.
Staleness detection: flag to user if last_updated is more than 7 days ago.
Chat widget uses nano-gpt API (same as playground) with full logs.json as system prompt. Key stored in localStorage under 'logs_ng_key'. No streaming — simpler for an information-retrieval tool.
CLAUDE.md updated to include a mandatory logs sync section — this fires at start and end of every Claude Code session.
logs.json update itself is included in the relevant session commit, not as a standalone commit.
The _protocol section encodes the schema_version increment rules so future agents maintain consistency.
rules_reinforced: logs.json must be updated every session — added to rules.absolute as a hard rule
The chat widget is client-side only — API key never leaves the browser except to the API endpoint
nano-gpt is the canonical LLM API endpoint for all client-side AI features on this site

sessions.summary: Full pre-production audit of the entire codebase. Fixed XSS in guide/index.html (DOMPurify + path validation). Fixed IntersectionObserver re-observation in art gallery. Deleted publicly-served detention.json.backup. Added **/*.backup to .gitignore. Updated CHANGELOG.md. Created logs system.
decisions: DOMPurify is already in config.shards but was never loaded on the guide page — added it to the parallel shard load.
Path traversal fix: regex /^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+\.md$/ is the right level of strictness for a filename whitelist.
The detention.json.backup being all-different IDs from current data confirmed it was safe to delete — not a corrupted current file.
Lucide icon names boom-box, swords, images, refrigerator are all valid — the audit agent flagged them incorrectly.
The backup file in art/glass/data/ was identified as the old 71-artifact dataset. Current 5-artifact file is a fresh replacement collection — intentional.
Logs page created as status/logs/ — reads a JSON knowledge base and renders it as an interactive briefing.
rules_reinforced: DOMPurify must be loaded on any page that renders user-reachable markdown
URL params used in fetch() must be validated with a regex whitelist
IntersectionObserver selectors should use :not(.visible) to avoid re-observing already-entered elements

sessions.summary: Underdark (characters/roleplay) major expansion. ECHOES data refreshed. Status page implementation. LLM playground updates.
decisions: Status page added as /status/ — live infrastructure monitor with sparklines and response times.
ECHOES collection replaced — new dataset, different medium types.
The Underdark: character editor, image generator integration, persona system.

for_new_llm.read_this_first: You are about to work on trap.lol. The file you are reading is status/logs/logs.json at the root of the trap.lol repository. It is the site's persistent memory and single source of truth. Read it in full before touching any code. The _protocol section is your operating instructions — it tells you exactly what to update and when. The rules and anti_patterns sections are load-bearing — violating them will produce work that gets reverted. Your last act in every session is to update this file (status/logs/logs.json) and commit it.
the_five_laws: 1. No frameworks. No build steps. Vanilla only.
2. All data lives in JSON. All HTML is scaffolding.
3. esc() on all JSON data going into innerHTML. DOMPurify on markdown.
4. lucide.createIcons() only after all HTML is injected.
5. The aesthetic is Dune × Arcane. Dark, glowing, precise, poetic. Never corporate, never default.
for_new_llm.the_sixth_law: 6. Update this file (logs.json) at the end of every session. Prepend to commits[]. Prepend to sessions[]. Update _meta.last_updated. This is not optional.
how_to_help_effectively: When asked to add a new section or item: edit the relevant JSON file. Do not touch the HTML.
When asked to add a new page: follow the pattern of the nearest existing page. Match its CSS variable usage, its glass morphism, its mono typography.
When asked to fix a bug: read the file top to bottom first. The architecture is intentional. The bug is almost never where you think it is.
When asked to improve performance: look for DOM thrashing, unnecessary re-renders, unscoped lucide.createIcons() calls, unscoped IntersectionObserver selectors.
When asked to improve UI: the answer is almost always 'add a glow' or 'add a sweep' or 'add an entrance animation'. The palette is fixed. Don't introduce new colours.
When unsure about tone for UI copy: terse. mono. ALL CAPS for states. lowercase for whispers. Never full sentences in labels.
At session end: update logs.json per _protocol before committing.
what_trap_values_most: Sovereignty — full control, no external dependencies beyond what's necessary
Precision — every pixel intentional, every byte justified
Aesthetic consistency — the palette and feel must be coherent across all pages
Speed of iteration — ship and refine, don't over-architect
Security at the boundary — validate user input, sanitize rendered content, expose nothing accidentally
Surprise — the gacha pull, the terminal shortcut system, the cursor orb — the site rewards exploration
Memory — the logs system exists because trap doesn't want to re-explain the same context every session. Honour it.
